commit.message:
Save git commit hash in model metadata (#176)

Adds the current git commit hash to the model save data so we can
trace which version of the code a model was trained on. The hash is
captured at save time and restored on load, and is included in
load_metadata() results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: per-test JSON config loading with encounter win/loss tests (#42)

Add infrastructure for loading custom JSON configs per test:
- config_loader: load_token_balances_from_json(), load_library_from_json_configs()
- game_state: GameState::new_from_json() constructor
- lib.rs: rocket_initialize_with_game_state() variant
- Test helper: create_test_client_from_json()

Add 17 focused win/loss encounter tests (9 win + 8 loss):
- Combat, Mining, Herbalism, Woodcutting, Fishing: win + loss
- Rest: win only (always wins by design)
- Crafting, Research, Milestone: win + loss
Each test verifies encounter outcome and ability to pick next encounter.

Add 21 minimal JSON test configs under tests/configurations/.

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: scouting mutation generates 3 mutated encounter variations (#41)

After completing an encounter, the scouting phase now generates 3 mutated
variations of the just-completed encounter as additional choices alongside
the normal encounter deck refill. Each variation applies a random difficulty
delta ([-0.15, +0.30]) to encounter parameters and mutates ~20% of enemy
deck cards while preserving total card count.

Key changes:
- New scouting.rs module with mutation logic for all 8 disciplines
- GameState captures last_encounter_kind in all discipline finish functions
- EncounterApplyScouting handler generates mutations + normal deck refill
- EncounterPickEncounter handler cleans up un-selected mutation cards
- 3 mutation operations: ScaleValues, RedistributeCopies, SwapTier
- Combat merges 3 decks for 20% selection; herbalism uses characteristic
  mutations; fishing also scales FishCard.value
- Updated tutorial, hints, and designer docs with scouting mutation info
- 3 new integration tests (136 total, 82.39% coverage)

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Milestone reuse discipline effects (#40)

* BREAKING: Milestones reuse discipline card effects with best values

Milestone encounters no longer define their own separate card effects.
Instead, they look up the discipline's existing EnemyCardEffects and use
roll_best_concrete_effect (max values) to create the most powerful
version of each effect.

Key changes:
- Add tier: u32 field to LibraryCard for tier-aware effect lookups
- Add roll_best_concrete_effect() that always picks max values
- Add Library methods: card_effects_for_discipline_and_tier,
  enemy_effects_for_discipline_and_tier, add_card_with_tier
- Refactor all 5 register_*_milestone functions to derive from
  the discipline's base encounter definition instead of hardcoding
- Combat milestones classify enemy effects into deck categories
- Gathering milestones (mining/herbalism/woodcutting/fishing) extract
  the base encounter structure and rebuild with best-rolled effects
- generate_milestone_reward_effects now also scales EnemyCardEffects
  and tags new effects with the next tier number

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add vision/roadmap suggestions for milestone effect reuse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve milestone deck composition on tier escalation

When a player beats a milestone and the next tier is generated, the new
milestone now mirrors the defeated milestone's deck structure instead of
rebuilding from scratch or from the base encounter.

Key changes:
- Add Library::replace_card() for in-place card replacement at same ID
- Generalize tier_effect_mapping() to accept from_tier/to_tier
- All 5 register_*_milestone functions accept optional previous def
- Combat milestone preserves per-deck card types and hand counts
- Non-combat milestones derive from defeated milestone (not base encounter)
- handle_milestone_finish extracts defeated inner encounter before replace
- Use scale_factor() consistently for tier scaling ratios
- Add 2 integration tests: deck composition + card ID preservation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove tracked dist/ binaries from git

GoReleaser treats any tracked-but-modified file as a dirty repo and
refuses to run. dist/ is already in .gitignore but these binaries were
committed previously.

commit.message:
Fix SLERP camera interpolation and address CI/review feedback (#1211)

* Initial plan

* Implement SLERP interpolation for camera path extraction

- Replace naive linear interpolation with mathematically correct SLERP
- Use scipy.spatial.transform.Rotation for quaternion-based SLERP
- LERP translation separately from rotation
- Add comprehensive test for rotation orthogonality preservation
- Fix cache path TODO in realesrgan.py (use ~/.cache/transformation_portal)

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback for SLERP test

- Add named constants for test parameters (TEST_RMSE, TEST_ITERATION, etc.)
- Add detailed comments explaining rotation matrix convention (Z-axis up)
- Clarify coordinate system used for camera extrinsics

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: Apply Black formatting and bump ML test ceiling

- Apply Black formatting to test_scene_utils.py
- Bump FAST_ML_SELECTED_CEILING from 69 to 70 to account for new test

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI lint: Apply Black formatting to scene_builder.py

Fixes implicit string concatenation formatting in NotImplementedError message

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments: Update ceiling docs and lazy-load scipy

1. Updated test_ml_fast_collection_contract.py docstrings:
   - Updated baseline from 69 to 70 tests
   - Added changelog entry for SLERP orthogonality test addition
   - Updated contract description to match actual ceiling

2. Moved scipy import to lazy loading in scene_builder.py:
   - Removed module-level scipy import
   - Added lazy import inside extract_camera_path method
   - Maintains docstring's "Lazy loading of dependencies" contract

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add release workflow (#27)

* feat: instruct LLM to output summary in Japanese

Update _ANALYZE_PROMPT to specify 'in Japanese' for the summary field.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add release workflow with auto-generated release notes

- release.yml: triggers on v*.*.* tags, runs CI then creates GitHub Release
  with auto-generated notes via softprops/action-gh-release
- ci.yml: add workflow_call trigger so release.yml can reuse CI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: address release workflow review comments

- release.yml: add secrets: inherit so CODECOV_TOKEN is available in CI
- release.yml: set prerelease/make_latest conditionally based on tag containing '-'
  (e.g. v1.0.0-rc1 -> prerelease=true, make_latest=false)
- ci.yml: change fail_ci_if_error to false so Codecov step is non-blocking
  when invoked via workflow_call without a token

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add goreleaser-based multi-OS release pipeline

- Add .goreleaser.yml with builds for linux/amd64, linux/arm64,
  darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64, windows/arm64
- All builds use CGO_ENABLED=0 (pure Go cross-compilation, no toolchains)
- Archives as tar.gz (unix) and zip (windows) with checksums.txt
- Changelog auto-generated from conventional commits
- Homebrew tap publishing via HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN (skip_upload: auto)
- Replace custom release.yml with goreleaser-action workflow
- Add scripts/install.sh for macOS/Linux quick install
- Add scripts/install.ps1 for Windows quick install
- Update verify_release_contract.sh for archive artifacts (6+checksums)
- Add CHANGELOG.md (updated automatically on each release)

Prerequisites:
- Create richardthombs/homebrew-prr repo with Formula/ directory
- Add HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (PAT with contents:write on tap repo)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

BREAKING: Add interference deck to research encounters (#39)

Add enemy interference deck to research encounters following the
established enemy deck pattern (OreCard, FishCard, etc.). The deck
has 5 cards in hand and auto-plays one random card per round after
the player's probe, adding difficulty to experiments.

Interference card effects:
- BlockBestMatch (2x): nullifies the player's best-scoring card
- SwapHiddenSlots (1x): permanently swaps two hidden slot symbols
- ReduceYield (1x): subtracts 20-80 from round yield
- ShuffleHiddenSlots (1x): randomly permutes all hidden slot positions

Implementation details:
- New ResearchInterferenceKind enum and InterferenceCard struct
- New CardEffectKind::ResearchInterference variant
- JSON-externalized definitions in configurations/research/cards.json
- Config types (ResearchDefConfig, InterferenceDeckEntryConfig) and
  build_research_def() loader following mining's pattern
- Borrow-checker-safe staged approach in apply_research_interference()
- 2 new integration tests (133 total, 81.72% coverage)
- Updated hints.rs and designer.rs documentation

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SurfaceNormal location mismatch and CreatePolyhedron deflection scaling (#6)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct SurfaceNormal location mixing and use relative meshing deflection

- SurfaceNormal: obtain surface + location via BRep_Tool::Surface(face, loc),
  transform query point into surface local frame before GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf
  so the (u,v) parameters match the BRepAdaptor_Surface domain used by
  TryGetOutwardNormal; delegate final normal computation to that helper.
  Remove the now-unused kMinNormalMagnitudeSquared constant.

- CreatePolyhedron: replace hard-coded kLinearDeflection = 0.1 with
  kRelativeDeflection = 0.01 and isRelative=true so mesh density scales
  with each face's bounding-box size rather than being a fixed world length.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ocr): enhance OCR capabilities with Vision LLM and Tesseract fallback

- Introduced Vision LLM for image text extraction, supporting any language and font without the need for language packs.
- Implemented a fallback mechanism to Tesseract for OCR when Vision LLM is disabled or fails.
- Enhanced PDF processing to handle both digital and scanned PDFs, utilizing `pypdf` for digital PDFs and OCR for scanned ones.
- Updated environment variables for OCR configuration, including new options for Vision LLM settings.
- Improved text extraction methods for images and PDFs, ensuring better handling of various file types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  pip dump, doubles priority, play strength, variety, blocking, partner respect

Add coaching mode with hand analysis and move suggestions

- Coach engine (src/engine/coach.ts) analyzes hand strength, scores moves
  using 6 heuristics (pip dump, variety, strength, partner respect, blocking, doubles)
- Only uses publicly observable info: player's hand, board state, turn history
- Tracks opponent passed values by replaying history
- CoachPanel component shows hand analysis, suggested move, alternatives
- Suggested tile gets cyan glow/pulse highlight in player's hand
- Toggle button (Coach ON/OFF) near speed sliders
- Panel appears only on human's turn when coaching is enabled

commit.message:
Fix six pip layout on horizontal tiles

Pip positions for 6 were two columns × three rows, which looks correct
on vertical tiles but wrong on horizontal ones (appeared as vertical
lines). Added a 'rotated' flag to getPipPositions that swaps x↔y
coordinates, so horizontal half-tiles show two rows × three columns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Show full game log with scrollable panel

Removed the 12-entry limit — all moves are now shown. Panel uses
max-height 40vh with overflow scroll so users can browse the full
history. Auto-scrolls to latest entry. Shows move count in header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch to HD MP4 videos, wider 2-per-row layout

- Replace GIF demos with autoplay MP4 videos (2MB total vs 20MB GIFs)
- 10 comparison demos in 2-column grid
- Wider layout (1200px max-width)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix passes not showing in game log

advanceToNextPlayer was skipping blocked players without recording
pass entries in turnHistory. Now it records a PassMove for every
player it skips. This also means human auto-pass is handled by the
engine itself, so removed the redundant manual auto-pass logic from
useGame hook.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make pass entries more visible in game log

Changed pass log entries from dim gray to orange with emoji,
making them easier to spot in the game history.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch to penalty scoring: pips penalize losing team, first to 100 loses

- Domino: losing team's remaining pips added to their own score
- Blocked: each team gets own remaining pips as penalty
- First team to 100 loses; other team wins
- Updated all UI to reflect penalty scoring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reveal all tiles and show pip counts at round/game end

When a round ends, all players' tiles are shown face-up so users can
verify the score. Round-over overlay includes per-player pip breakdown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix board chain pushing East player offscreen

CSS transform: scale() doesn't affect layout size, so the flex
container saw the full unscaled chain width. Added min-w-0 +
overflow-hidden on board wrapper, and an explicit-sized wrapper div
reflecting scaled dimensions (naturalWidth * scale).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial release: Visual Chronometer project page

- 10 PhyFPS correction demo GIFs
- Project introduction and key findings
- Citation section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add AppHost launchSettings for Aspire endpoint discovery

Aspire reads the project's launchSettings.json to discover HTTP/HTTPS
endpoints to proxy. Without it, no endpoints appear in the dashboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(Types): convert constructor inline PHPDoc to @param format (#51)

* Initial plan

* refactor: convert inline constructor PHPDoc to @param format in src/Types/

Co-authored-by: kawax <1502086+kawax@users.noreply.github.com>
